3715802:
Shay Citadels

The Shay Citadels is a gnome monastery established on the island of Irrere, in the Ironbound Archipelago of the Steaming Sea, soon after their exile from the First World. It is located at the base of a waterfall next to the town of Irrere.

The Citadels consist of a number of towers with twisted spires of multi-coloured glass that rise above the waterfall's mists. The monastery was established for research and meditation, and is a regular destination for gnome pilgrims, especially those suffering from the Bleaching.

The monastery sometimes temporarily phases out of existence. Upon returning, all of its buildings remain intact but no gnomes except for the Bleachlings return with it.
19992782:
Starwatcher Tor

The Starwatcher Tor was a tower and armory built in the Terwa Uplands in collaboration between Azlant and Ghol-Gan, but most of the structure has collapsed. A xacarba was imprisoned in the cellars, remaining dormant for millennia until it began to awaken in 4716 AR, manipulating researchers in the region.
25961422:
Lions' Square

The Lions' Square is located in the Foreign Quarter and is one of the largest enclaves in the district where Taldor expatriates can live in peace. After every war, famine, and political conflict, the district receives new migrants, and with the prohibition of faith in Sarenrae by Stavian I, many of the inhabitants are devout followers of the goddess. After the War for the Crown, many nobles, exiled or dissatisfied with the new direction of the country, have come to enjoy the freedom that Absalom offers.
28207972:
Nybor

Nybor, founded in 4641 AR, is perhaps the most racially accepting settlement in Varisia and ranks highly among the list in all of Golarion. Located on the southern shores of Ember Lake and bordering the Sanos Forest, the peaceful farming community has a more half-breeds per capita than any other Varisian city. Interracial marriages are not only tolerated, but promoted, making it a haven for those who find themselves in precarious situations involving illicit pregnancies and other affairs which might be judged harshly in more puritanical locations. Being so close to the Sanos Forest, Nybor is also home to a sizeable gnome population.
31291582:
Droskar's Crucible

Sleeping in the southern shadow of Droskar's Crag on the edge of Darkmoon Vale lies the now-ruined monastery once known as Droskar's Crucible. At its prime, the monastery was home to dwarven clerics of the malevolent god Droskar, who urged them to ever toil in making weapons on their great forges. The evil forge spurned which came as a result of their worship eventually drove the dwarves out of their haven, and left behind a now-haunted shell of a building. After its fall to decadence and decay, legends began to spread among the dwarves telling of vast riches now lost beneath the crumbling citadel. The greatest dwarven hero, Druingar the Glintaxe, ventured into the depths below the once-sacred site in search of secrets and the ancestral gauntlet the Grasp of Droskar, but he was never heard from again, and inhabitants of the Vale still talk of his ghost haunting the ancient ruins. In the dungeons beneath, the Truescale Kobolds now make their home, ever balanced between their desire to delve deeper into the earth and their fear of what frightening beings lie in wait for them there.
33371292:
Fort Scourbone

Fort Scourbone is a location in northwestern Geb about which little is known. It is located on the western edge of the Shattered Range near the Mana Wastes.
37406702:
Hekastor

Hekastor was a city and golemworks of the Jistka Imperium known to be active ca. -3600 AR. It was destroyed and buried by earthquakes of divine nature millennia ago. Golems still roam the site, and its former residents slowly evolved into morlocks who remain active in the area. These Jistkan morlocks obsessively work to power 17 glowing magical pylons out of fear of releasing a destructive threat. The morlocks have been unable to power the pylons by any means other than the sacrifice of arcane magic spellcasters beneath the pillars.
45927622:
Whitespectre Canyon

Whitespectre Canyon is a box canyon in the Nesmian Plains of Nirmathas. Numerous predators have claimed the canyon for themselves. As of 4717 AR it was plagued by Witchpelt, a giant smilodon that escaped from a traveling Varisian circus.
51968412:
Ohira

Ohira is the Spirit Road's largest village and serves as a popular intermediate destination for adventurers seeking to journey deeper into the Forest of Spirits.
60326262:
Immaculate Path

The Immaculate Path is a bridge connecting the Everlasting Palace to the rest of Kerse. It is a structure filled with fountains and golden statues of prophets of the past. Its open-air state might suggest a lack of defense, but that would be a misconception, as many of the statues are actually guardians that awaken to defend the bridge if it is invaded.
60749332:
Chapel of the Argent Shield

The Chapel of the Argent Shield is an Iomedaean temple located on the road between Kenabres and Egede. The chapel's walls are marked by many shrines and tributes honoring the fallen Mendevian Crusaders, as well as art mapping the region's numerous battlefields.
66309272:
Gulgamodh

Gulgamodh is a gargantuan construct that once stood guard over the Precipice Quarter of Absalom. Centuries ago, after an earthquake, Gulgamodh fell down an expanding sinkhole which was later filled in as part of an urban beautification initiative, leaving only its right arm visible on the ground. An architect, unaware of the arm's significance, later built a fountain around it, which came to be called Titan's Fountain by locals. Along the fountain's surface is displayed this text:
79091402:
Khitai

Khitai is a lively city in the Tian-Shu nation of Quain, on Tian Xia, that is well-known for its many festivals and dancers that attract swathes of foreign visitors. These dances are based on various creatures including dragons, lions and basilisks.
80351242:
House of Shade and Grace

The House of Shade and Grace is a popular Thuvian-style inn in the Foreign Quarter of the city. Built of sun-dried brick, it serves food that evokes memories of home for its guests. As one might expect, thuvians in the city, such as Lord Yamthar, are heavily involved with the establishment, lending their clockworks to assist with the service.

Katapesh

85735992:
Cave of Tiandra

The Cave of Tiandra was the cave complex where Tiandra hid the Azlanti relics she pillaged in the later years of the Age of Darkness. Generations later, Arustun discovered and miraculously interpreted these relics, which enabled the rise of the first human civilization after Earthfall: the Jistka Imperium.

Most Jistkan scholars agree that the legendary Cave of Tiandra is located northeast of what is now Manaket on the Inner Sea coast of Rahadoum. The cave appears to be nothing more than a hillside grotto with a single barren chamber and its low ceiling eroded. However, a narrow pitch in the back of the entrance cavern reveals that beneath the initial level lies a vast network of tunnels, dens, and hollows, with soaring drops and huge domes.

The presence of cullitox has led the scholars to believe that the Cave of Tiandra once had a link to the Plane of Earth, and perhaps to other planes. This might explain how Arustun gained the miraculous insight from the relics found there in -4160 AR.
90361282:
Sampadabad

Sampadabad is a ruined city in the north-west of Vudra's storied central region known as the Cradle. Sampadabad was not a Vudrani city but rather one built by nagas.
100753882:
Citadel of the Supreme Elect

1 locations:
  • Archstone Monument
106755272:
Planepierce Spire

The Planepierce Spire is a tower filled with laboratories and warehouses, all belonging to Cyran Androtus. Only those invited by him can enter; the doors are only opened rarely to obtain supplies from the outside. There are rumors that extraplanar creatures are also bringing supplies from other planes inside, and given Cyran's involvement, this is quite likely.
108171512:
Zura's Mouth

Zura's Mouth is a series of springs and pools in the Kaava Lands and the source of the slow and broad Oubinga River which meanders through the jungles of the southwestern Mwangi Expanse before emptying into Desperation Bay. A number of tribes worship the place and use the pools for ritual sacrifice by drowning. The bodies don't last long, as the Oubinga's upper reaches are home to crimson maka-yika, a schooling, carnivorous fish similar to piranhas.
126190652:
Oagon

Oagon was a city in the now-lost kingdom of Lirgen. Its location at the mouth of the Black Flow river (in what is now known as the Sodden Lands) ensured it was completely covered by floodwater on the advent of the Eye of Abendego in 4606 AR.
131388712:
Hisuikarasu

Hisuikarasu (pronounced hee-soo-ee-Kah-rah-soo) is the capital of the tengu nation of Kwanlai, in Tian Xia. A relatively prosperous city in chaotic Kwanlai, Hisuikarasu is ruled by the half-celestial benevolent autocrat Lady Sutarai-Gongen. Lady Sutarai-Gongen's popularity is such that her power is beginning to spread beyond the capital to the rest of Kwanlai.
134352522:
Famished Mind

The Famished Mind is an entity that resides within a citrine sphere of unknown origins, hanging from an overpass in the Kortos Mounts. It was discovered decades ago by a clutch of xulgaths who found a cache of orange crystal shards humming with hunger in their home, and followed the trail of increasingly big crystals, believing this to be a sign from their patron Zevgavizeb. When the xulgaths approached, they were consumed, mind and body, by the Famished Mind, which gained the ability to affect and bind basalt in the process. Now the Famished Mind builds orange and grey xulgath simulacra and sends them ranging across the Kortos Mounts. These simulacra seek to bring prey to the Famished Mind to consume, allowing it to grow in knowledge and subtlety and spread its enigmatic agenda.

Westcrown

Yargos's Mission

146138502:
Wedding rock

Wedding rock is a ruined ancient Azlanti altar on the island of Nal-Kashel, in the Hellmouth Gulf west of Cheliax. Residents of Blackcove, a village on the gulf's coast, traditionally consummate their marriage vows on the altar.
160220532:
Jundlan Wrecks

The pirate lords of Issia (now part of Brevoy) once engaged one another in a massive naval battle on the Lake of Mists and Veils. During the battle, the fleets were caught up in a massive storm and wrecked off the coast of Brevoy in Jellicoe Bay. The wreckage remains there to this day, and is known as the Jundlan Wrecks. Presumably a considerable amount of pirate treasure can be found amongst the frost-covered tangle of wrecked ships and ancient, waterlogged corpses. However, numerous unfriendly fey now live amongst the destruction, building altars and structures made from the dead pirates' treasure. On moonless nights at midnight, ghostly bells toll here and gangplanks made of bone extend from the shipwrecks and invite all to board.
162826412:
Prophet's Home

The city of Prophet's Home is located in the northeast of the country of Druma. It is best known for housing the training facilities of the Mercenary League, the military protection and enforcement arm of the Prophets of Kalistrade.
163849052:
Cindermaw's Feeding Grounds

Cindermaw's Feeding Grounds are the ancient home of the purple worm Cindermaw. Located in the Cinderlands of Varisia, it is here that the Shoanti hero Skurak faced Cindermaw with only a dagger, was swallowed, and cut his way out from its fiery belly.
178080632:
Urfa-Halij

Urfa-Halij is an ancient memorial located in the Sophra Prefecture and is sacred to both the people of Taldor and their neighbouring rivals in Qadira. Urfa-Halij marks the location of the first ever battle between the people of Taldor and, as it was at the time, the Empire of Kelesh. Known as the Battle of Urfa to the Taldans and the Battle of Halij to the Qadirans, Urfa-Halij recognises both perspectives and stands as a testament of respect for those soldiers lost by both sides of the war. Both sides recognise this place as sacred and, by custom, no sort of conflict takes place for miles around the monument. This has made it one of the most peaceful and fertile areas along the war-ravaged border between Taldor and Qadira, with rabbits being particularly common.

The Urfa-Halij monument itself consists of two raised arms, one wielding a Taldan-styled longsword, the other a Qadiran scimitar, the blades of both meeting in the middle where a huge diamond is set. This diamond catches even the dimmest of light, making it seem as if the blades are sparking even on a moonlit night. Many debate, due to the striking nature of Urfa-Halij, if it is really a monument to the dead or if, in fact, it is secretly a tribute to the glory of war.
191775822:
Green Ridge

Green Ridge is a neighborhood in the Eastgate district of Absalom. It is one of smallest and least populated parts of the city. It contains a number of extensive gardens and parks, as well as several copses of trees scattered throughout it, that inspire the district's name. Green Ridge also contains many small farms a result of past sieges. In contrast to much of Absalom, the district tends to be peaceful and quiet.
194842232:
Merry Widow

The Merry Widow was a ship that was scuttled off the southern coast of Slitherfish Island to the northeast of Mediogalti Island. It is now half beneath the waves; its water-filled lower decks are home to great numbers of sharks and devilfish.

As of 4711 AR the wreck was being used as a temple to the pirate-goddess Besmara, established by a sect known as the Merry Widows. This splinter group is made up of women who have lost their husbands at sea. It is said that some Mediogalti women donate unwanted males to the ship-temple as a sacrifice to the goddess.
204585332:
Iarna

Iarna is a small town on the Marbleflow River in the Irriseni province of Wintercrux. The town is a popular destination for Jadwiga and rich foreign merchants because of its famous hot springs. There are three resorts catering to the aristocracy as well as expensive restaurants and two grand theaters.

The hot springs began bubbling up some 200 years ago and are thought to have magical and medicinal powers although nothing has been proven. Hundreds of ageing or sick Jadwiga bathe in the hot springs every year. The truth is that a trapped and insane magma elemental heats Iarna's springs. The elemental was summoned by druids of the Heralds of Summer's Return 200 years ago but was captured and bound by Iarna's baroness at the time. As of 4713 AR, the ruler of Iarna was Baroness Iracetta, who knew the truth but preferred not to damage the town's businesses. The ageing Iracetta was a strong advertiser of the springs' therapeutic qualities.

Jazdya Vacalav, captain of Iarna's town guard, often fights fire and magma scamps who are attracted by the insane elemental.
207137872:
Padiskar

Padiskar was once known as the Shimmering Jewel of Jalmeray, when it was a city famous both for pleasure and for arcane learning. The Arclords of Nex found a nameless horror in the vaults beneath Old Padiskar, and today the city is reduced to being a community of farmers and fisherfolk. The city rings the ancient ruins, surrounding but never entering them. Whatever awful thing the Arclords released still roams the darkened depths below Padiskar, howling in the blackness.

Unlike many other old ruins, Padiskar has yet to be reclaimed by the Thakur, and the locals whisper that there may be some fell reason for this. Some have even claimed the Arclords released a bound deity which had been transported to the island by Khiben-Sald thousands of years ago.
222038842:
Elidir

Elidir (pronounced EL-eh-deer) is both the capital of Isger and the nation's largest city. Far from the Chitterwood, Elidir escaped the worst of the Goblinblood Wars, a fact that still engenders bitterness from those whose homes were overrun. It is a commonly held belief throughout the rest of the country that if the capital had been in greater danger during the war, the fighting would have been over much faster.
235831372:
Caruskei

Caruskei is a ruined temple to an unknown god, located in the far-eastern Fierani Forest of the elven kingdom of Kyonin, less than 50 miles west of Kallas Lake.
238168732:
Pex

The pirate town of Pex is located on the Devil's Arches island of the Shackles, at the south end of the perilous Straits of Balthus. It is a rough, ramshackle settlement, ruled by the capricious Hardluck Massey. He is known for his impulsive punishments against the unfortunates who have annoyed him. Pex boasts a large three-story inn called Damned If It Didn't Rain run by Hegmar of Frembrudd that gathers buccaneers of Ulfen descent as well as Varisian merchants who own warehouses in the town.
246884592:
Trillia's Bathhouse

Trillia's Bathhouse is often one of the first stop for sail crusted sailors or weary desert travels, it sits on the dock of Katapesh and is easily recognised by its sign of a cooper tub above the door. It offers over a dozen private bathing chambers, a basic bath cost one silver piece for fifteen minutes so a traveller can bath for two hours for less than a gold piece additional soaps and amenities are available for more. Unlike many other bath houses Trillia's, still run by Trillia herself, does not offer any sort of lewd services. It is however a hotbed for information as many travellers loosen their tongues in the relaxing waters of the bathhouse.

Red Pyramid

254664302:
Moundlands

The Moundlands, located near the confluence of the Whitegold and Vyalldehun Rivers, is where the early Linnorm Kings were buried. Here, scores of small burial mounds surround 99 larger hills, 50 feet in height, each one of which contains a buried burial complex. The larger hills house the graves of the Linnorm Kings, while the smaller ones contain their champions or lesser nobles. The area is now avoided by all, as the kings animated in their cairns after the 99th one was laid to rest here.
256257502:
Arodennama

The Arodennama, a massive statue of Aroden, stretches 90 feet above its base on Aroden's Rise in Westcrown. Created from massive blocks of white marble, it is the oldest structure in Westcrown and, perhaps, even in all of Avistan. The statue faces the river and Westcrown Island, with the chest aligned to stand if the first rays of sunlight in the morning and the last at night. A massive silver-inlaid winged-eye symbol of Aroden stretches to the sleeves and under the arms.

A variety of structures of many ages surround the statue, originally built in the expectation of his arrival, most now lie neglected and vacant. In contrast, even after a century's neglect, no signs of tarnish or wear mar the silver eye of Aroden across the breast. Within the statue are a number of chambers originally designed as places to pray and pay homage to Aroden. Most troubling to many of his clergy is that no residual magic or lingering curse remains here from Aroden's demise. After all, if Aroden could not even leave his blessing here, the place where his prophesies foretold he would return to the world, then his demise is even more definite and profound.

The area surrounding the statue is a large plaza known as the Vacant Throne, and encompasses several buildings (now abandoned). The area has been described as having a feel of ancient but abandoned majesty.
261781042:
Alyssia's

Alyssia's is a very expensive but extremely beautiful teahouse in the Ivy District that caters to elven tastes and is run by Lady Alyssia. The hallways are curved, giving the impression of an aiudara, and although most of the staff are elves, any customer is welcome as long as they have the money and the decorum to behave within the establishment. Lady Alyssia often calls on the Brotherhood of Abadar to deal with troublemakers and remove them from the premises.
288909352:
Monastery of the Seven Forms

The Monastery of the Seven Forms is an underground city built around a Tian monastery, located in the prefecture of Whitemarch in the nation of Taldor. This settlement is nestled in the World's Edge Mountains, and overlooks the lands of the empire all the way to the Inner Sea.
300962412:
Pendaka

Pendaka is a tiny thorp located on a rocky promontory that overlooks Claybottom Lake's southern shore near where it flows into the Skull River. Pendaka has a single inn that also functions as a trading post known as the Walleyed Wife, but the settlement's only real claim to fame is its skilled baker, Olam Keecher, and his delicious cranberry turtle egg pies.
307748612:
Crooked Cove

Crooked Cove, often referred to as Crookcove or Crook Cove, is a small village on the southwest coast of Ravounel. It houses an abandoned observatory.
313835502:
Osprey

The Osprey is one of the ten remaining pilings of the Irespan in Magnimar. It is the shortest of the ten, standing only 100 feet high.

There is an entrance into the upper piling. The church of Abadar is rumored to operate a prison here. Locals believe that political prisoners and others that are best hidden away are held here.
318180432:
Barterfall

Barterfall is a neighborhood in the Precipice Quarter, named for its economy and precarious living conditions. It's one of the least dangerous parts of the district, though that's not saying much. Its inhabitants are the descendants of those who were either too poor to move or wanted by the law, and many don't even have jobs, earning their living by fishing, trapping for vermin, and farming whatever they can. Violence and disease are common, and the mansions converted into apartments are falling apart. While its residents want to move to other districts, they also resent the rest of the city for abandoning them. There's an enclave of Kalistocrats who trade coin for what the residents scavenge from the ruins, making them the object of envy and hatred from the inhabitants who barely see them behind their walls and clean houses.
331997272:
Thornkeep

333119652:
Three Rings Tavern

The Three Rings Tavern is a tavern in the Five Corners district of Korvosa that belongs to a retired Pathfinder named Theandra Darklight. Theandra is a good-natured bartender and is always hungry for any tales of adventure or daring-do. The Three Rings Tavern is comparatively quiet, serving as much coffee as it does alcoholic beverages. The Three Ring's bouncer Tauk Par keeps a close eye on things and barely manages to conceal his dislike for all of the taverns clientele. As well as a wide range of drinks, the Three Rings Tavern is renowned for its breakfast services which include local staples like Varisian sweetbreads all the way to exotic pastries whose recipes come from distant Geb.
373539332:
Datorro's Brewery

Datorro's Brewery is an extravagant stall in the Daystalls, the shantytown of vendor stalls, tents, and kiosks in the city of Katapesh. Ano Datorro sells casks, bottles, skins, and mug samplings of his various ales. His workspace is full of still and alchemist's equipment and smokes all day long, churning out a variety of ales.
377338282:
Myscurial

Myscurial is a small town on Widowmaker Isle in the Shackles. It is built on Sweet Tooth Lake in the island's interior and hosts naval gladiatorial games between captives or poor debtors armed with simple spears in rowboats. The games become more interesting because of the unique beasts brought into the aquatic arena by mayor Salty Chuzek, which include fiendish lukwatas, giant toads, snapping turtles, and an unbeatable, ravenous catoblepas.
377841512:
Queen's Processional

The Queen's Processional is a location one must pass in order to enter the Cove of the Queen on route to the mythical Holy Isle found in the Shackles island known as Besmara's Throne.
392551002:
Dyinglight

Standing at the headwaters of the Sarkora River and surrounded by the marshes and sulfurous hotsprings of the Frostmire Fen, Dyinglight was once a major city in the Kellid nation of Sarkoris. Abandoned by its human inhabitants shortly after the opening of the Worldwound in 4606 AR, Dyinglight is now the home of a particularly violent band of fiendish, cannibalistic marsh giants.
395247682:
Old Sehir

Old Sehir is a small town in western Taldor, approximately 90 miles northwest of the capital city of Oppara.
399833262:
Abberton

Abberton is a small, declining town in the eastern part of the Isle of Erran. It is debated whether the town's name is shortened from Abadartown, or if it was named after the founding Abber family.
400666352:
Petronax Estate

Petronax Estate is the Petronax family home, made up of three multi-story structures. Vayde Petronax, the family patriarch, rarely leaves this estate.
429894652:
Wyrmwatch

3 locations:
  • Battle of Charda
  • Left-Handed Lobster
  • Outcast Fishery
436139182:
Monastery of the Unremitting Tide

The Monastery of the Unremitting Tide is historically an Iroran monastery on the eastern coast of Nex.
455807482:
Guidestar of Desna

The Guidestar of Desna is a haunted lighthouse and former temple to Desna located on Phahalen Island's southern cliffs. Despite being unmanned, its light still serves as a beacon for ships traveling around the Eye of Abendego.
461147192:
Mantis Stone

The Mantis Stones are a series of menhirs outside the city of Ilizmagorti, which are linked to the predatory forces that dominate the island. When the Achaekek cult arrived on the island and found that these monuments were linked to giant mantises, they saw it as a sign from their god that they were on the right path by relocating to the island.
471459112:
Uddo

Uddo was the capital of the Teikoku Shogunate in what is now known as Minkai. It was conquered and razed by the armies of Kuga Setsuna during the Battle of Eight Bridges in 6116 IC; Minkai was founded three years later, with the downriver port city of Kasai as its capital.
481717982:
Emperor's Pass

Emperor's Pass is a mountain pass in the northern edge of the Southern Range mountains of Taldor, directly north of the town of Tribulation.
484786652:
Fangwood Keep

Fangwood Keep is a fortress near the Nirmathas–Molthune border in the Marideth River valley. Ownership of the fortress has alternated between the two nations since the beginning of the Nirmathi war of independence. It was built on top of the ancient tower of an elven wizard.
484867052:
Gozreh's Pool

Gozreh's Pool is located in the Mwangi Jungle, north of the Ocota River. It is 15 feet across and is nearly a perfect circle. The unusually cool and clear pool is home to a pair of water elementals, which require that the pool is shown the proper respect—typically evidenced by some form of prayer or sacrifice—before it is approached.

A dip in the pool is refreshing, and also reputed to cure any disease or parasitic infection contracted in the jungle.
498794682:
Driftwood

Driftwood is a small town in the coast of Katapesh sheltered from the weather but with little trade thanks to its ragged terrain.
503489452:
Greenblood

The Free Captains of the Shackles established a pit fight on a rock in the sea just off the coast of the Sodden Lands located northwest of Neruma. It became known as Green Blood on a Black Rock, and its fame spread as far as Avistan.

The popularity of the event led to the settlement of Greenblood growing up close to the site. Its population of brigands are considered to be rough and lawless even by Shackles standards.
507443372:
Mount Onik

Mount Onik is the 10th-tallest peak of the Five Kings Range at 14,714 feet. It is one of four mountains surrounding the city of Taggoret, alongside Mount Soryu to its west, Mount Langley to its north, and Mount Carissa to its south.

520726642:
Wart Rock

Wart Rock is the smallest of the islands surrounding Cutlass Cove, its most notable feature being the black sand that makes up its beaches.
520979422:
Citadel Vaull

Citadel Vaull, located in the Castle District of the city of Kintargo in Ravounel, housed the otherwise nomadic Hellknight Order of the Torrent until 4715 AR, when new Lord-Mayor Barzillai Thrune stripped the order of its charter and had the Order of the Rack loot the citadel of its contents.

After Barzillai Thrune was removed from power and Kintargo and Ravounel won their independence from Cheliax in 4716 AR, Citadel Vaull was returned to the Order of the Torrent, who began renovating its facade to be more approachable. However, work has been slowed by the Order's simultaneous work caring for Castle Kintargo.
525187492:
Besmara's Boardwalk

Besmara's Boardwalk is located in the eastern part of The Docks, where visitors can participate in carnival games, purchase souvenirs of their visit to the city, or enjoy street food from the various food stalls. One of these stalls features a knife-throwing game with a symbol on the wall of two crossed swords, recognizable as the symbol of the Bladed Bastards, a local Firebrands group.
526376672:
Castle Odranto

Castle Odranto is located in the centre of the Ustalavic county of Odranto.
541972792:
Redtooth

Redtooth is the provincial capital of the Irriseni border province known as the Verge.
560484082:
Skywatch

The abandoned city of Skywatch is built around a massive observatory high in the Icerime Peaks on Brevoy's eastern border. Once a holding of House Surtova, it was mysteriously sealed for 20 years before being reopened and revealed to be abandoned in 4722 AR.
564262602:
Obari Imports

Obari Imports is located in the center of Mainmast, offering goods from all over the world. Its owner, Sanjeet Ralama Pakhesh, comes from Jalmeray and has connections with Absalom, Katapesh, Nex, Vudra, and even Tian Xia. Custom orders can be placed for a fee, and he is always protected by his Vudrani monk bodyguards.
564497632:
Mother of Skulls

The Ustalavic county of Barstoi boasts three Pharasmin monasteries: the Chapel of Guilts; the Mother of Skulls; and the Vault of Tears. The Mother of Skulls lies in the south-east of the county between the Evergray and the West Sellen River near the border of Ustalav with Numeria.
573304662:
Wondervale

The Wondervale was created decades after Aroden's death by Absalom's intellectuals, who sought to showcase the city's inventions and demonstrate that it had not declined in relevance after the death of its patron god. They chose Eastgate and Green Ridge as the venue for they were the least populated areas with the largest open spaces, and proceeded to construct illuminated bridges, Tian Xia-style towers, Vudra pagodas, and Casmaron palaces. The resulting intellectual and cultural exchange was a resounding success, drawing attention to the nation. After the festival, the structure was abandoned, and the escaped plants and animals give the site a sense of ancient ruin, a place favored by lovers, poets, and followers of Brigh. The most striking features are the Jalmeray-style flying towers, teeming with bats.
575570872:
Erages

Erages is a small fishing community located on the shore of Lake Encarthan in the elven nation of Kyonin. Erages' population is almost entirely aiuvarins who live in virtual exile from the rest of the country.
584720962:
Saffron House

The Saffron House is a crumbling mansion once owned by Cleid Thord located within the murky Sclerain Swamp in eastern Canterwall, one of the counties of Ustalav. There is something very wrong about this haunted and abandoned home: it is claustrophobic inside, the sheet-covered furniture is mouldy and there are noises from within the walls. Many of its room seem stunted or underformed, while others seem cripplingly small. Even stranger is a repulsive yellowing on every surface. Not many know of this house and fewer understand its blighted nature.
587549122:
Vigil's Rest

Vigil's Rest is a small outpost near the border with the Whistling Plains in Avin Prefecture. The Taldan Horse offers no protection beyond this point, and at least one regiment can always be seen in the area. Although Vigil's Rest is not especially populated, the local market is well supplied as it is on a main trading route. In addition to the Taldan Horse, the Lion Blades have members at the outpost who interrogate a good number of caravan workers that arrive from Casmaron.
588740542:
The Three Furies

2 locations:
  • Well of Sorrows
  • Wizard-King's Pit
590294592:
Lost Yemal

Lost Yemal is a ruin of the ancient Jistka Imperium located in the nation of Rahadoum. It is situated in the south-east of the country, in the Napsune Mountains near the mouth of the Uta River.
592169982:
Redleaf

The settlement of Redleaf is located in the western portion of the Ustalavic county of Varno, circling the small Lake Redleaf.
620605442:
Tamran

The city of Tamran, capital of Nirmathas, is a ramshackle city, especially for a nation's capital. It is built on piers and pilings over the marshlands at the mouth of the Marideth River, where it meets the banks of Lake Encarthan. The city is connected by a web of bridges, ramps, and causeways, and a flotilla of barges and skiffs, and all of the buildings are made from wood. Due to the fact Tamran is still standing after its violent history, it is seen as a symbol of Nirmathas's tenacity and resourcefulness. Considering Nirmathas is a country at war, Tamran seems to lack any sort of defences, including a city wall—but as history has shown, this is in fact its best defence.
623142472:
Weeping Stairwell

The Weeping Stairwell is a hidden series of caverns on the eastern coast that provide access to the isolated upper reaches of the secret island of Grand Sarret.

The Weeping Stairwell can be accessed from the waters of the Obari Ocean via a cave that lies concealed below the waterline along the eastern reaches of Grand Sarret. The Weeping Stairwell can also be accessed from the island's top via a narrow fissure hidden within the dense foliage of Grand Sarret. The Weeping Caverns gained their name as a small stream cascades down the through the caverns making the climb up from the ocean even more treacherous. To make matters worse for any trying to traverse the Weeping Stairwell, the masters of the Conservatory, the Jalmeri training school for spies that sits atop Grand Sarret, are well aware of the Stairwell's existence. As such they have placed a pair of faydhaan to guard the Weeping Stairwell to prevent intruders from accessing the island.
649321772:
Surat Prakan

Surat Prakan (pronounced sue-RA-ht pra-KHAN) is the traditional capital of Tang Mai, home of its ruling kamraten and their Cinnamon Throne, and is the Tian Xia nation's largest city.
665135312:
Cayden's Arm

Cayden's Arm is a hilly cape on Taldas Isle in the Shackles near the town of Little Oppara. It once was a destination for the citizens of the nearby settlement, who came there to have fancy dress parties and picnics. That changed when the cape was claimed by a minotaur during one of these extravagant holidays. The creature was eventually beaten back to its lair by the pirate lord Petrina Maxeme, but it is feared that the minotaur might soon reappear to once again claim the territory.
669100022:
Slither Cove

Once a reasonably prosperous village, Slither Cove is now nothing but a sinking ruin being rapidly consumed by the surrounding swamp. Slither Cove's current name comes from the wide variety of snakes, lizards, and other reptiles that now inhabit it.
676911892:
Fair Fortune Livery

The Fair Fortune Livery is a horse stable in the Redroof neighborhood of Kintargo, Ravounel. As of 4715 AR it had been abandoned and fallen into disrepair, but by 4724 AR it had been recently reopened by the Delronge family.
708054462:
Czarny Las

The small town of Czarny Las sprang up around the Bloodstone Conservatory in the middle of the of the Irrisen province of Feyfrost. The town and school sit atop a conjunction of arcane energy.
720498032:
Halabad

Halabad is a town on the Rampore Isles in the Shackles, inhabited mainly by slaves who are born and raised there. The town is full of ramshackle housing complexes and equally decrepit breeding centers, where slaves are forced to intermingle and create more slaves. Brutal trainers in the town's training camps teach children at an early age to perform the tasks for which they are made for, breaking their backs and spirits.

726107312:
Palin's Cove

Named for Keyra Palin, naval heroine of early Korvosa, Palin's Cove is one of the most important holdings of the city-state's realm. Originally a military outpost, Palin's Cove played a vital role in early Chelaxian victories over the Shoanti. Located approximately 80 miles northwest of Korvosa at the mouth of the Falcon River, Palin's Cover served as a naval garrison until 4485 AR, when it became an official settlement of the city.

Over the years, a number of extensive Shoanti sieges were laid on the town, but it never fell, and was rewarded by Korvosa with a strong industrial presence. Modern-day Palin's Cove can be identified for miles around by the cloud of thick black smoke which rises from its many factories. The pollution caused by the industrial epicenter of the town have caused great animosity and even violence from druids, Gozreh worshipers, and even ordinary smiths and craftsmen pushed out of their niche in the market by the overwhelming workhouses.

Not only do the catapults, ballistae, and trebuchets it produces serve as major exports both for the town and Korvosa, but they remain ever ready for another attack, a remnant of the town's military origins. Despite these defenses, Palin's Cove has long been plagued by the red dragon Glarataxus, who has periodically tormented the town since his first attack in 4571.
727628782:
Gralton

The inhabitants of Gralton consist almost entirely of people who have fled the ongoing revolution (some would say anarchy) in Galt. Many of those who have fled to Gralton are the old nobility of Galt. Deposed and fallen from grace, they desperately seek any means to regain what they have lost, be it by sponsoring counter-revolutionary activity, or sending parties of hapless adventurers back into Galt to search for family heirlooms left behind in the rush to escape the guillotine's blade. As the desperation of the deposed nobility grew, Gralton began to attract con-artists and tricksters. These tried to hustle the desperate nobility by offering quick fixes and false hope. The town has two notable temples: one to the drunken hero deity, Cayden Cailean, the other to the Vengeful Sting, Calistria. In fact, many suspect the people of Gralton to be possessed by some spirit of Calistria. So bent on vengeance against Galt are they, that many see divine intervention in their hate-filled eyes.
734271882:
Rumbutter

Rumbutter is the southernmost settlement on Bag Island in Shackles. It is a sprawling, hilly, farming community known for its apiaries and honey. Rumbutter honey finds its way throughout the Shackles and Sargava. Rumbutter's three taverns on its main road, Bedda's Berth, the Gilded Lily, and Shopin's Roundhouse, serve unique blends of iced butterscotch beers in the rainy season and honey wine in the drier months.
734925942:
Sanctum of Sand

The Sanctum of Sand is a mysterious location in the desert of southern Katapesh. It is believed to be located inside a valley within the Trackless Storm and is the origin of the Decanter of Black Breath artifact.

Tastikka

Tastikka is a small village of the strix Kitkasiticka tribe in the Ravounel Forest. They traditionally isolated themselves from human contact until the defeat of the Grundlescorn sisters encouraged them to pursue careful and cautious negotiations. The tribe's rokoa Rarrnir, who came to power after the abdication of her predecessor Solmestria, had met with representatives from the Silver Council as of 4719 AR but as of that time had yet to secure an agreement concerning the new settlements along the Ravounel Forest's borders.

Bailax

Bailax is the capital of the city-state Pol-Bailax of Iblydos.

764800422:
Navah's Tower

Navah's Tower is a structure built into the side of a cliff located 20 miles from Longshadow in the Hollow Hills. Navah created the tower using transmutation spells, and the area radiates this type of magic. Another tower and a basement accompany the main structure, which is well-positioned for defense.
765900242:
Starrise Spire

The Starrise Spire, the tallest building in Nerosyan, is located on the city's outer wall in the Confluence District neighborhood of Coopertown. It is topped by a celestial light that continuously shines in even the darkest of nights and never fades as long as at least one defender still resides in the tower. This beacon provided hope and relief to adventurers that crusaders still stood against the demons of the Worldwound, and continues to operate as a center of restoration efforts since the Worldwound's closure into the Sarkoris. Before the Pathfinder Society was tasked to protect the city, the city's crusaders defended its walls.

The Starrise Spire's venture-captain is Jorsal of Lauterbury, a well-decorated Mwangi man with over 50 advances into the Worldwound under his belt. Jorsal delegates divine magic training and consecration of the Spire's holy sites to Seeker Ereth Brightmane.
766167722:
Elesomare

Elesomare is a small farming and lumber-producing colony, established in pre-revolutionary times by the then province of Andoran on the far-away shores of Arcadia. As it was overwhelmingly settled by commoners looking for a better life abroad, it greeted Andoran's radical change in government with great hope and sympathy. Being separated from the motherland and the many political and economic compromises that a large government necessitates, the citizens of Elesomare are more idealistic than their counterparts in Avistan. In recent years, the colonists have been attempting to decrease the local influence of the authoritarian and sometimes brutal Lumber Consortium. Although the government in Almas does not support this subtle insurrection, they have been noticeably slow in responding to the Consortium's call for help and reinforcements.
781455962:
Ahn'Selota

The mysterious pyramid of Ahn'Selota, also known as the fabled Pact Stone Pyramid, is located near the ruins of Tumen in the Scorpion Coast region of northeastern Osirion. The pyramid is somehow linked with the magical pact that united the Four Pharaohs of Ascension more than 6,000 years ago. It is said that Ahn'Selota houses the secret of the pharaohs' powerful pact that joined their fates.
785022942:
Blacknape Hideout

Blacknape Hideout is located on one of the islands in the Westchannel, where a gang of thugs, released from prison in 4710 AR by the Council of Thieves, gathered to wreak havoc. The group specialized in kidnapping children and the elderly, who were easy to hold prisoner and could demand ransoms for their safe return.
788821492:
Menhu Leng

Menhu Leng, also known as Menhu Suu, is a ruined city high in the northern Wall of Heaven mountains of Tian Xia. Menhu Leng sits in the central portion of the mountain range, an area that is known for its links to the nightmare plane of Leng. The cause of this link is much speculated upon, some believe this is due to the height of the mountains tearing at the very fabric of reality itself, while others speculate that in the most distant reaches of the past the denizens of Leng worked to create a primal link with the mountains. Whatever the case, the denizens of Leng now rule over the basalt ruins of Menhu Leng, using their winged shantaks to visit across the lands of Tian Xia to further their sinister schemes.
801450742:
Brunderton

The mining town of Brunderton in eastern Rostland in Brevoy is home to a relatively large number of dwarves for the region. Most dwarves throughout southern Brevoy and the northern River Kingdoms have relatives from here, and gem and ore traders from Brunderton can often be found peddling their wares along the Sellen's many tributaries.

857089962:
Katapesh Lodge

The Katapesh Lodge, the Pathfinder Society's local base of operations in the city of Katapesh, is an elegant three-tiered building located among cyclopean ruins and other ancient marvels in the Twilight Gate district. Venture-Captain Aurora Steelbloom oversees the operations of several teams of Pathfinders.

The lodge maintains a library full of field agents' chapbooks, historical treatises, maps, rare tomes, and other items. The Pactmasters agreed to post two aluum outside the lodge for its protection. The building itself is thoroughly warded against any intrusion.

There are three other lodges in Katapesh: Farseer Tower Lodge, Kotargo Lodge, and the Winding Road Lodge.
859544222:
Crooked Falls

The Crooked Falls are a series of five waterfalls, each dropping by 10 to 30 feet, over a one-mile course in the northern Stolen Lands section of the Shrike River. Its challenging features prevent river trade between Brevoy and lands to the south, with most trade traditionally using the East Sellen River through Hooktongue Slough instead. The Crooked River flows into the Shrike around half-way through this cascade of waterfalls.
862311432:
Droskar's Crag

Droskar's Crag is an active volcano located in the Five Kings Range. The mountain consists of two primary peaks known as the Hammer and the Anvil. The Hammer reaches 28,822 feet in altitude, while the Anvil—which hosts the active crater known as Rovagug's Caldera—is 28,305 feet tall and grows by about one and a half feet per year. These heights make Droskar's Crag the tallest known active volcano on Golarion and the Five Kings Range's tallest mountain.

Several rivers originate or are fed from glaciers on the Crag, including the Coldforge River (from the Coldforge glacier), the Slagiron River (from the Slagiron glacier), and the Goldmelt River (from the Gold glacier). The crag also hosts the East Gold, Sunset, Silver, Peakcrest, Eastrise, and Deaddrop glaciers.

The volcano last erupted in 3980 AR in a massive explosion known as the Rending, which destroyed the capital of Tar Khadurrm at Jernashall and transformed the region. At the time Droskar's Crag was known as Torag's Crag; its name was changed after the Rending to reflect the more hostile nature of the volcano, since the evil dwarven god Droskar is the patron of endless toil and suffering. To date, it has shown no major activity, though the peaks constantly spew ash and smoke into the air as a constant reminder of the threat that exists beneath the snow-capped mountain. Prior to the eruption that changed Torag's Crag into Droskar's Crag, this volcano had only one peak. The eruption in 3980 AR formed the two peaks of Droskar's Crag that are now known as Hammer and Anvil.
863184052:
Castle of the Captive Sun

The Castle of the Captive Sun in Nidal is home to the vampiric nobleman Volsazni Dezarr. The castle is primarily known for housing a very large collection of holy artifacts.
863937132:
Cerulean Spiral

The Cerulean Spiral is a large chasm of nested ruins curled inward like a gigantic nautilus several miles wide and just as deep, located in the Holoma province of Tinhri on the Obari Ocean coast. The area has been the site of many small skirmishes among bands of adventurers fighting for control of and loot in the area, to the point where some believe the area itself drives them to such violence.
866963042:
Dorjanala

Dorjanala is a palace built in 4336 AR by order of Queen Dorjana to serve as her magnificent city residence, featuring solariums with crystals constructed from metal plates on the sides. In 4716 AR, a descendant of the monarch, the knight Bellinia Dorjana, took possession of the site for the Glorious Reclamation.
879847452:
Freedom Town

Freedom Town is a predominantly human settlement located on the border of the Hold of Belkzen and Lastwall. It is located in fertile farmland due east of the capital of Urgir, north of the Flood Road.
893234472:
Shelyn's Bow

Shelyn's Bow is an irregular geyser that blasts boiling water and steam 800 feet high. The ejected colorful water carries all manner of minerals. The geyser can be seen up to a mile away and serves as an unreliable landmark. It shoots water roughly four times per week, and the height of its spray has increased over the years.

Murkfen

982275312:
Beast

The Beast is the largest crane in the world. It is located at edge of the Long Pier, near the center of the Docks district of Absalom. The Beast is used to unload the heaviest and largest loads in Absalom. It is a five-story stone windmill-like massive structure, with a long wooden winch-and-rope system sticking out of the top. The entire building can rotate on its axis and its main arm can extend out from its normal 25-foot length. Built 500 years ago, it is well maintained, capable of lifting nearly 60 tons to a height of 55 feet. When lifting loads no more than 10 tons, it can swing them in a 360-degree arc anywhere within 100 feet of its central tower. Its structure contains 16 human-operated treadwheels. All the warehouses built around the Beast are multi-storey buildings with central shafts to allow the Beast's cables to raise and lower cargo to any of their floors.

Responsible for the operation of the Beast is the First Harbormaster. Running the Beast requires engineering expertise, thus the crane is hired out to a professional crane master. Recently, Adrielle Neprathep of House Fyrlenn was replaced by Amaziah Meneha as the chief engineer of the Beast.

In 4499 AR, during the Red Siege, the Beast defended itself, using a one-ton block of stone as an oversized flail.
1000832242:
New Stetven

The city of New Stetven is located on the southern banks of Lake Reykal (also known as Needle Lake) in central Brevoy and serves as the nation's capital. The city has been the seat of power in Brevoy since the warlord Choral the Conqueror founded it over the ruins of the Taldan settlement of Stetven in 4499 AR after uniting the separate nations of Issia and Rostland under his rule.

New Stetven is a bustling trade city that acts as a hub for exporting Brevoy's rich supplies of grain, fish, timber, and ore to the rest of the northern kingdoms, such as Mendev and Numeria, and down the East Sellen River to the River Kingdoms. Food from Rostland sustains many of the northern realms, where constant bandit raids or invading demonic armies make farming difficult. In exchange for these much-needed goods, imports of exotic items from all corners of Avistan and beyond, such as rare Numerian skymetals and ancient Osiriani relics, are common in New Stetven's rough-and-tumble marketplaces.

The city, which has made prodigious use of wood in constructing everything from mansions, to streets, to forts, is also known as "The City of Wooden Palaces." Due to the prodigious number of wooden structures within its walls, the city boasts several volunteer fire brigades that protect its buildings—citizens join these brigades mostly out of a sense of civic pride. There are but two notable stone structures in New Stetven: the Ruby Fortress, which houses the Dragonscale Throne sits, and the Bulwark of Gorum, a temple to the Lord of Iron.

Snow and slush cause the streets to turn to mud for most of the year.
1006907832:
Burozi

Burozi is a small island in the Steaming Sea west of Varisia that was rediscovered by the Pathfinder Society ca. 4720 AR. The island contains ruins of the Azlanti Empire.
1009777362:
Eternal Shackle

The Eternal Shackle is a 20-foot-tall stone arch monument at the northernmost edge of Misery Row in Absalom. A massive magic flame burned beneath the arch. Locals say that the flame did not burn for decades until the Flesh Taxes were reinstated in 4635 AR, legalizing slavery once more. From time to time, a hermit or madman comes to the monument to venerate Droskar, the Dark Smith and god of slavery.

In 4717 AR slavery was abolish and the flame went out greatly angering the followers of the Dark Smith that have petition the city to light it up themselves bu the followers of Cayden Cailean are making things complicated by writting the names of those that lost their lives before archiving freedom in the stone. Unbeknownst to both groups the stone that was used for the monument was taken from the Brazen Arch the gate Voradni Voon used to arrived in the island and the minotaurs of Hazrak are waiting to use it to invade the city.
1027655522:
Tanadesh

Tanadesh is an unusual mahajanapada in the Cradle region of Vudra. Unlike most mahajanapadas composed of Vudrani, Tanadesh is a kingdom of vishkanyas, humanoids who possess a supernatural affinity for poison and guile. It is a jungle kingdom located toward the centre of the Cradle.

Emissaries from Tanadesh travel seasonally to Radripal for study in the city's ashrams.
1038565002:
Vonosh

Vonosh was founded in 3314 AR after the Winter War to serve as the provincial capital of Hoarwood. In 3444 AR, Queen Morgannan razed the city to the ground in a storm of fire and ice and executed all residents of Vonosh—regardless of guilt or innocence—for their part in a plot to overthrow her and in retribution for the death of her daughter.

Following the Hoarwood Rebellion in 3445 AR, the Heralds of Summer's Return tried to use the ruins of Vonosh as a base for guerrilla strikes against the new provincial capital city of Hoarwood. The plan failed because of the angry, restless spirits of those who had been executed by the queen. Some of the Heralds did not escape the attack of the spirits inhabiting the ruins.
1041748272:
Witchlight Vale

Witchlight Vale is a crater in western Numeria formed by an explosion that occurred nearly 7,000 years ago.
1075814302:
Hapkani

The town of Hapkani was famous for being the location of Hakan Mutalib's Clockwork Vault, situated on a nearby plateau, but after a sandstorm and subsequent desertification of the nearby farmlands have done damage to the city's population. The town is slowly dying, being buried by the sands with fewer and fewer inhabitants each year.
1094153912:
Castle Corvischior

Castle Corvischior (once known as Korsinoria Palace) is a seemingly abandoned fortress near the village of Corvischior in the eastern Ustalavic province of Varno. Situated near Lake Korsinoria, it was once the county seat of Varno's ruling family, House Tiriac. The count is believed to have abandoned his home long ago to go gallivanting abroad, ignoring the needs of his people. In his absence, rumors of hauntings have begun to be told about his home: people see strange lights at night, or see gates open to admit unmarked carriages. The truth behind these rumors, is that the castle is still inhabited by a Tiriac: the enigmatic Counte Risotmaur. Unbeknownst to most of his citizens, he has lived there for over two centuries using a variety of guises, as he is, in fact, a vampire.
1097488232:
Pestico's Dolls and Figurines

Pestico's Dolls and Figurines is a shop located in the south of the Midland district that sells dolls and figurines to the delight of children all across the city of Korvosa. The shop is run by Vadid Pestico: a kindly, but fragile, old man, who builds the store's dolls. His late wife, Kiristi, used to sculpt the figurines, until her death over a decade ago. Recently Vadid unveiled a project he had been working on for a long time: a group of six soulbound dolls he named "Pestico's Daughters". These 'daughters' help Pestico run the shop and have become quite the draw for tourists and locals alike. However, recently one of these dolls has gone missing and, shortly after she disappeared, a string of bodies were found nearby. So far, the Korvosan Guard has kept these killings under wraps, as the victims were vagrants who have yet to be missed.
1106885212:
Bellezeph Villa

2 locations:
  • Oakrib Inn
  • Veil of the Spire
1107740942:
Mistshroud Cliff

Mistshroud Cliff is in the eastern reaches of the Eismonts mountain range, south of the Maiestas River in southwestern Cheliax.
1110066112:
Spiralcross Cemetery

6 locations:
  • Ahnkamen Estate
  • Crescent Manor
  • Erastil Club
  • Fiddlemourn Manor
  • Kin Gate
  • Magpie Manor
1123157942:
Fountain of Tabis

Countless adventurers have set off into the Mwangi Expanse in search of the fabled Fountain of Youth. It remains undiscovered (unless legendary Pathfinder Durvin Gest actually managed to find it).

However, the Fountain of Tabis in the north eastern Mwangi Jungle does grant youth—at a price. Whoever drinks from it dies and is reincarnated as a young child, and not necessarily of the same ancestry or gender, with very little memory of their previous life.

The locals do their utmost to prevent outsiders from drinking from the fountain but adopt any who succeed and raise them as part of the tribe. Especially honoured tribal members may be allowed to drink from the fountain when they become old or infirm.
1128545502:
Orolo's Quay

Orolo's Quay is a nearly forgotten seaside town on Nidal's Conqueror's Bay.

Before the founding of the Varisian city-states of Korvosa and Magnimar, it was the last port before the long voyage through the wilderness to the cities of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. These days, the town and its Chelaxian fortress dating back to the Everwar have fallen into stark neglect, as very few ships dock here anymore. The only ships who make anchor at the crumbling wharf are those either desperate for supplies, or are smugglers bringing goods into Nidal who wish to avoid the harsh authorities in Nisroch farther south.

The first floor of the fortress has been converted into a shabby inn and trading post, with an attached stable of little note. Recently, rumors have begun to circulate that smugglers pay handsomely to store contraband in the fortress' old dungeons.
1133544132:
Emerald Spire

The Emerald Spire is a strange ruin in the northwestern River Kingdoms. Located in a large clearing in the Echo Wood near the Crusader Road, six miles northeast of the Hellknight-ruled settlement of Fort Inevitable, the spire is well known to locals and travellers in the region.

Believed to be an Azlanti ruin from the era before Earthfall, most think the Emerald Spire refers to the ruins of large tower of green glass that rises above the clearing. However, the spire itself is far more ancient, a needle of magical crystal that reaches two miles into the earth, into the very vaults of Orv.
1136617342:
Riba

Riba is a small town on the Marbleflow River in the Irrisen province of Hoarwood.
1152149302:
Spiral Shrine

The Spiral Shrine is a temple of Pharasma that was among the first to be rebuilt in the Precipice Quarter after the earthquake that shook the district. Hundreds of candles were placed in its alcoves to honor the memory of those whose bodies could not be recovered from the rubble, and the residents of Beldrin's Bluff donated significantly to consecrate the temple and enable them to hire undead hunters. It is one of the few places in the district where divine magical healing can be obtained, and the abandoned mansions in the area have been converted into apartments where a community of duskwalkers lives, fighting the undead. For this reason, the district's living residents hold them in great reverence, bowing their heads when they see one or offering them food and water when asked.
1156646422:
Drowning Stones

The ruins of the city of Drowning Stones, located in the northern Mwangi Jungle of the Mwangi Expanse, were abandoned by its population when it began to sink into the swamp. It sits about halfway between the Olongo River to the north and the Vanji River to the south. Today, only the central fortress remains relatively dry. The city takes its name from the massive standing stones that served as pillars for the central forum.
1170946662:
Chídanza

The Ruins of Chídanza is all that remains of the city of Chídanza after the nearby river dried up in 4622 AR. Located in the north-western reaches of the Arcadian nation of Xopatl, Chídanza used to be the nation's greatest farming settlement, providing food for much of its citizens. In 4622 AR, the Arpretzí River began to mysteriously dry up and, over the following years, more and more farmers could not provide for themselves and moved away, leaving the city abandoned. Nowadays, the ruins of Chídanza are overrun with magical beasts of many kinds, though a few hardy farmers who are able to grow their crops without the river's water still linger on.
1177085632:
Aphet Mines

The Aphet Mines are three interconnected copper mines in the Footprints of Rovagug. They are some of the few existing mines that have not been completely depleted by the Pharaohs' demands for weapons and ornamentation.

The mines are owned by the Aspis Consortium and overseen by Falsin Deek. Under the operation of Deek, the mines produce consistently high output, and also operate as a front for the Consortium's movement of goods and people into and out of Osirion.

The nearby town of Aphet East provides support to the mine's workers.
1194616212:
Acisazi

Acisazi is a small aquatic elven shoreline village in Ravounel, north of the Ravounel Forest. It was a part of Cheliax until 4717 AR, when the Archduchy of Ravounel seceded and became an independent nation.
1198602862:
Castle Kronquist

Castle Kronquist is a haunted Ustalavic fortress that is home to one of the Whispering Tyrant's most dreaded vampire minions, the vampire lord Malyas. The castle is located amid the fog-wisped woods of northern Odranto. Its infamous fanged gates have remained closed since the fall of the Whispering Tyrant, centuries ago. Tangled vines climb its walls and overgrown thickets and brambles cover the surroundings, but the castle has not deteriorated such as an ancient site should have. Each month on the night of the new moon, its great iron-horned clock tower rings. Rumors persist that something lurks within, and the rumors are supported by sightings of swiftly moving shadows and strange lights flickering from the castle's lonely towers.
1217084552:
Maidenspool

The town of Maidenspool once vied with Drenchport for dominance of the island of Tempest Cay in the Shackles. Free Captain Bradesmar Wache of Mediogalti poured enormous resources into the town, building structures of stone and fine lumber. At the peak of Maidenspool's growth, Bradesmar openly boasted that the Master of the Gales's city would soon be just so much driftwood. Quickly after Bradesmar's proclamation, Maidenspool was attacked by swarms of athamarus. Most of the town was destroyed by fire and hundreds of its residents were killed, including Bradesmar's darling wife Ella Wache.
1226194742:
Kraggodan

The dwarven city-state of Kraggodan can be found in the southern Mindspin Mountains within the borders of the nation of Nirmathas on its western border with the nation of Oprak, just north of the ruined village of Graybanks
1231649062:
Stavintower

Stavintower is a town in eastern Galt, just south of the Boarwood who is quite isolated from the rest of the nation.
1237367842:
Voluse

Voluse is a small town in the River Kingdom of Touvette, located on the confluence of the Yhalt and Pitax Rivers. It is the site of one of Touvette's three charity buildings, along with the capital of Avendale and the town of Seredain.
1252795872:
Whistledown

The town of Whistledown is one of the few settlements in Varisia not affiliated with either Korvosa or Magnimar, though it is located almost exactly halfway between the two larger cities. Founded at the western edge of the Fenwall Mountains, where Lake Syrantula flows back into the Yondabakari River, the town is known primarily as the heart of the gnome population in Varisia, despite the fact that it contains just as many humans as gnomes. Nevertheless, much of the town's buildings and its white-walled cottages are sized for gnomes. Its name derives from the strange wooden charms which produce haunting melodies in the night winds off the lake, and this nightly music tends to put off most non-gnome visitors.
1276045662:
Fort Indros

Fort Indros is located at the north-easternmost point of Magnimar, amongst the riches of the Alabaster District on a spur of land that juts out into the Varisian Gulf. The fort is named after its designer Alcaydian Indros, the founder of Magnimar and a watchful paladin of Aroden. The fort gives an unmatched view of the surrounding Gulf and serves as a deterrent to any pirates (from Riddleport or anywhere else) who might think of attacking the city. The fort bristles with siege weapons and bowmen. As of 4707 AR, it was overseen by the dwarven commander Vanns Wynmerd.
1278148112:
Auginford

Auginford is a small village in southern Nidal near the southern Usk River.
1278829322:
Cuttle

Cuttle is a small settlement on the west coast of Mediogalti Island. It is much smaller and less well-known than the island's main settlement of Ilizmagorti.

It consists of a collection of huts on the edge of the beach and serves as a way station for the whalers of the Arcadian Ocean. The whalers pay the Red Mantis for the use of the site, normally in the form of whale oil, and several of the villagers are Red Mantis members in disguise. The whalers resupply here and also render the catches.
1291129852:
Castle Penatha

Castle Penatha was originally built in the World's Edge Mountains in Qadira, but disappeared down to its foundation around 4691 AR. Its fate remained unknown until it suddenly reappeared completely intact—with the addition of a tower with a golden cupola—near the summit of Mount Rectzaid, in the mountains of the Ustalavic county of Ulcazar. It is now said to be the home of Isla Bellsan, the purple-cloaked, self-proclaimed greatest thief in Avistan.
1334057512:
Church of the Muted God

The Church of the Muted God is dedicated to this would-be god of the Failed, who perished during the Test of the Starstone but still has followers. The Muted God provides no divine power or guidance, and the members of this benevolent organization are becoming more of a criminal group than a religious congregation.
1343467222:
Kintargo Opera House

2 locations:
  • Crissali's Fine Tomes
  • Olmer's Smithy
1359469592:
Chesed

The huge city of Chesed (pronounced CHES-ed) is the last port of call on the River Road and the final port (or first, depending on the direction of travel) along the length of the Sellen River, located where the river meets the Lake of Mists and Veils. The city has a blend of alien technology, as well as stark social inequality, resulting in peoples' perception of the city being very different depending on whom they asked.
1376398002:
Fort Riverwatch

Fort Riverwatch is a small town located ten miles northwest of the village of Thornkeep, on the banks of the West Sellen River. It is the last settlement of the River Kingdoms before one crosses into Numeria or Ustalav.

The town serves the barge traffic along the West Sellen. After Mosswater's destruction, Fort Riverwatch is the only port along the river between Lockridge, more than 100 miles to the south, and the muddy fishing town of Dravod Knock, 250 miles to the north. The knights of Iomedae established a garrison at Riverwatch in 4623 AR, and assumed the task of protecting the area. The knights govern the settlement and keep the river and roads safe for travel although they don't have the strength to clean out the lawless village of Thornkeep.
1382002872:
Dawnfoot

16 locations:
  • Blue Tower
  • Beldrin's Tower
  • Vigil's Hope
  • Grislyfair
  • Spiral Shrine

  • and 7 others…
1382271382:
Al-Hiraf

Al-Hiraf is a Qadiran city in the south-western corner of the Maharev region, one of the safest and most fertile regions in all Qadira. Al-Hiraf itself is located on the eastern bank of the Maharev River a few miles upriver of the metropolis of Qaharid.
1397241852:
Fort Ursoss

Fort Ursoss is an abandoned fort on the south bank of the Marideth River in the interior of the Fangwood. The swampy terrain makes travel on foot difficult and serves as a defensive feature. The fort dates back to the Shining Crusade era and was restored by Cheliax after its independence. Nirmathi soldiers burned the fort to prevent it from falling into Molthune's hands, though the stone basement still stands.
1402074812:
Windsong Abbey

Windsong Abbey is a pacifist refuge for followers of all religions—good and evil—to come and resolve their doctrinal and political differences in a non-violent manner and to peacefully further their own goals.
1403397532:
Peshaka Naeu

Peshaka Naeu is a small settlement of jungle giants in the deepest part of the rainforest of the Devil's Arches island in the Shackles. The giants peacefully raise crops and livestock as long as the humanoids of the island leave them undisturbed. Scholars believe the jungle giants have an oral history that goes back to the days of ancient Ghol-Gan but the giants have never accepted any diplomatic mission.
1410940952:
Lamasara

Lamasara is the third largest of Thuvia's five allied city-states and is situated on the Junira river. Its ruler, Queen Zamere, is known for her clever diplomacy.

Lamasara is famous for its performers, artisans, and hedonists in general, and is popular with tourists year-round. It is said that there is always music in the air, although the most well-known of its artists usually perform in whichever city is currently hosting the sale of the sun orchid elixir.
1427640382:
Bug Harbor

The town of Bug Harbor is a gathering of huts perched on stilts at the edge of swamp Evergrowth. It got its name from the swarms of dragonflies, water striders, and other insects that buzz over the swamp. The town supplies herbs and plants for alchemists, herbalists, wizards, and druids who travel here in search of rare components. The unofficial mayor of Bug Harbor is the aiuvarin Bastin Irriden.
1451446442:
Eilwith Glen

Eilwith Glen is a valley filled with bushes and ancient trees deep within the Verduran Forest. It is named after the druid Eilwith, a member of the Goroth Lodge and lover of Ghorus.

For centuries, the dweomercat Felivaine has lived in the valley. He was Eilwith's companion and is considered the guardian of the place where she once lived.
1463139752:
Aysepir's Astounded Abyss

Aysepir's Astounded Abyss is a carnival show of unusual, and often disturbing, beings in the Puddles district of Absalom. Despite having the trappings of fiction and making extraordinary claims, its employees are exactly what they are presented to be—beings of sometimes immense power afflicted by curses or misfortune, or simply being of unique parentage.
1465453742:
Jeggare Light

The Jeggare Light is a lighthouse located in Varisia where the Jeggare River spills into the Varisian Gulf, just across from the city of Korvosa. Jeggare Light is operated by Veranada Mezzerae, who was recently transformed by a cult of Urgathoa into a type of vampire known as a penanggalen. After assuring her compliance, the cult ordered her to allow the free passage of the plague ship Direption into the harbor of Korvosa.
1466875002:
Palace Ormuz

The Palace Ormuz is a white Thuvian-style structure where Scion Lord Yamthar invites the aristocracy to seminars where they can speak with researchers, scholars, and intellectuals, listening to or testing their theories. The south wing houses the laboratories where dozens of alchemists work and where Yamthar keeps his collection of Jistkan artifacts, which he has acquired and attempts to reactivate, including those that are normally dormant.
1487738612:
College of Dimensional Studies

18 locations:
  • Golden Scarab
  • Golden Oasis
  • Zulran's Equines
  • Zandrek's Pesh Palace

1489545192:
Birdcruncher Cave

The Birdcruncher Cave shelters the Birdcruncher goblins of the area from the elements. Located in the Devil's Platter, this cave has been used by many different people over the years, making it an ideal spot to survey the rest of the region.
1498148402:
Ostergard Manor

Ostergard Manor is located in the northwest of Cheliax, near the Menador Gap. Although easily accessible by road, no one dares visit the manor due to rumors that it is haunted. House Kastner lived here, acquiring diabolical lore even before the rise of House Thrune. The Kastners abandoned the manor and moved to Egorian when they no longer needed to conceal their research and devil-binding. The estate passed to the Blakros family in 4712 AR after Damian Kastner married Michellia Blakros.
1500895332:
Charred Meats

The Charred Meats is a butcher shop located on the edge of Dog Town, selling scraps of meat from other butcher shops, which the owner, Chargut, sells to fishermen and farmers. The establishment's secret business is selling corpses, with humanoids fetching the most money, and several disguised undead guard the shop at all times.
1501537102:
Bazaar of Sails

5 locations:
  • Captain's Club
  • Carent's Camp
  • Eyes of the Hawk
  • No-Horn
  • Old Fang
1509828872:
Stormspear Keep

The Stormspear Keep of the Blackravens is located at the northeastern corner of the region of Hagreach in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. It is a complex of towers perched upon a cliff nearly 2,000 feet high at the northernmost edge of the border between Hagreach and Irrisen. The Blackraven warriors are allied with many nests of giant eagles, who help them patrol the northern borders.
1513403132:
Tomb of the First Battle

The Tomb of the First Battle is a monument and cemetery where the native giants of the region that would become Belkzen rest. They fought against the orc hordes invading their territory. The orcs won what they called the First Battle, and as a sign of respect for their dedication to the fight, they allowed the giants to lay their dead to rest in this acropolis.

A dirt path branching off from Deepgate leads to a cavern filled with warnings that no orc should proceed further. Inside, the giants rest, protected by Ogon, one of the warriors who survived that fierce battle.
1514158022:
Kabirabad

Kabirabad is a ruined underground labyrinth located in the north of Vudra's storied central region known as the Cradle. Kabirabad is haunted by ghosts that date back to the Age of Destiny.
1514913442:
Corentyn

8 locations:

Matten Cleave

1542547372:
Gilded Orrery

The Gilded Orrery is a magic shop located in the Heights District of Korvosa. Gasta Weagra is the owner of the Gilded Orrery. A plump woman with gray hair, Gasta is the niece of Toff Ornelos, the headmaster of the Academae, the city's most prestigious school of magic, and rarely sells to anyone not connected with it.
1546319512:
Eyes of the Bronze Man

1 locations:
  • Pyre Crest
1550925472:
Cawshax

Cawshax, originally Cow Shacks, is an agricultural village in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos. It is located south of Potter's Lake at the crossroads of the Merovan Road and the roads connecting to Castinlee and Turpin Rowe, with a locally known but obscure cow path leading directly to the road to Kerrick. Both farmers and herders use the lands around Cawshax, with barley and millet as its crops and cattle as its livestock.
1568822492:
Ayesh

Ayesh is a city on the Alavah Peninsula in Qadira.
1570047312:
Southwestern Road

The Southwestern Road is made of dirt and leads into Kyonin where several human-run farms increase the food selection available in Greengold.
1586082022:
Ytterjorna

Ytterjorna is a trading town on the eastern shores of Glacier Lake in the Irriseni province of Thronehold.
1588991212:
Hell Harbor

1590850692:
Mount Yogan

Mount Yogan is a volcano in southern Minkai along the Kyojin Mountains, near the source of the Sakehiyaku River. It is one of the Three Fires, along with Mount Kumijinja in the Kamifushi Mountains and Mount Kazunan in the central Kyojin Mountains.
1591976112:
Westgate Heritage Museum

The Westgate Heritage Museum is dedicated to telling the history of the district from its beginnings to the present day, and is criticized by residents of other districts for the clear bias in its tours. Those with local knowledge and respect for traditions are offered jobs at the museum, and there's even a souvenir shop where gorgon figurines have become very popular after the Charge of the Gorgon was banned. One can dine at a restaurant serving rather delicious ancient foods, although the museum has problems with vandalism, paying well for those who bring the vandals to justice.
1597118412:
Talípas

Talípas is a city in the Arcadian nation of Xopatl that is renowned for brewing and distillation. Once Talípas was little more than just another sleepy fishing village sat along Xopatl's western coastline but, in the last few centuries, it has instead embraced distillation and grown in size enormously. Various lake plants found on Lake Tapipallati, which surrounds the island Talípas is built on, can be crushed into a paste that is then fermented for several weeks. The drink created from this fermentation, also called "talípas", is highly intoxicating. The exact process of creating the drink talípas is such a closely guarded secret that the brewers and distillers of the city are willing to kill to ensure it remains secret.
1600432462:
Nyshire

Nyshire is a small town in the Archduchy of the Heartlands between the capital of Egorian and Halmyris.
1611900062:
Jitai

Jitai (pronounced gee-TAI) is a city located in the former heartland of Imperial Lung Wa in the Successor State of Po Li. The nation is ruled by the powerful Oracular Council that allows no worship of other gods other than that of the idea of the Eternal Emperor.
1622229892:
Veka

Veka is an Iobarian town located on the shores of the Nyvyrd and has stood as a garrisoned fort since its founding. The easternmost Iobarian settlement in the Ice Steppes, it is home to a number of smithies, where all metal weapons and goods in the region are forged. The owner of these smithies is the ruthless Koffar Rjul, who holds control over the entire Nyvyrd region.
1628136922:
Hangman's Harbor

Hangman's Harbor is a village along the Sellen River in Taldor, north of Cassomir. The village is located between the Sellen and a large granite cliff, and it covers not only the riverfront but the top of the cliff, and the cliff itself. Some of its buildings jut out over the water, some are wedged onto narrow ledges, and some are carved directly into the cliff face. Because of the lack of flat land, the village is very built up, with individual buildings often being shared by multiple businesses. The mayor's house boasts an ominous noose that prominently hangs over the river. The town is home to a tavern called The Hanging Bottle.
1651831602:
Scarwall

The immense gothic fortress of Scarwall is located in the far-eastern reaches of the Kodar Mountains within the Hold of Belkzen.
1653133992:
Nisroch

Nisroch is the largest city in Nidal and also its most open. For most of the port's visitors, that means very little. The few ships that dock here are closely inspected, and the streets are heavily patrolled. Finding little in the way of traditional port entertainment, most foreigners spend as little time in the company of the city's suspicious inhabitants as possible.
1657677452:
Riddleport
