1668129982:
Gold mine

A gold mine lies about half-way between the northeastern edge of the Narlmarch Woods and the Shrike River to their south, where a rugged rock formation rises sharply from the hills. A 5-foot-wide fissure at the base of this formation expands into a 20-foot-wide gap, followed by a 30-foot-wide cave leading further below. Gold ore veins dimly sparkle from some of the cave's walls.
1669841542:
Grayce

Grayce is an Ustalavic settlement in the south-west of the county of Caliphas with the border of Lastwall, the Path River, a mere stone's throw away. This proximity means the town is a bridge to Lastwall with the Chapel Bridge, a drawbridge that can span the Path, literally fulfilling this role. From the southern end of the bridge, roads lead to Vigil, north-west of Grayce, and to Vellumis to the south.

The townsfolk are predominantly farmers, shepherds, and weavers.

Cader Boneyard

1696417062:
Sanctum of the Sorrowmaker

Sanctum of the Sorrowmaker is a labyrinthine cavern complex in Dahak's Fang island that Aashaq the Annihilator and her minions call home.
1723187122:
Elyon

Elyon is a settlement on the western coast of the Isle of Kortos in the nation of Absalom. It is located in the western Swardlands just west of the town of Kerrick.
1729348242:
Oe-Tet

Oe-Tet is a dwarven settlement in the Brazen Frontier area of Osirion. The Pahmet dwarves created this settlement to facilitate trade, without allowing foreigners into their other settlements. Traders are grudgingly let into the settlement, provided they do not stray too far from the bazaar and inns.
1735427412:
Western Lighthouse

The Western Lighthouse at Devil's Elbow was destroyed by fire shortly after its completion, and although the inhabitants of Witchlight intended to rebuild it someday, that moment never came, and it remains infested with insects that have moved into the ruins.
1738326062:
House of the Senses Awakened

The House of the Senses Awakened was the decadent project of Grand Prince Cydonus III, the ruler of Taldor who was so profligate in his spending that, during a mere five-year reign (4077–4081 AR), he nearly bankrupted the entire country. Cydonus spent his life wracked with anxiety over the possibility of an attack from neighbouring Qadira. He dealt with this fear by indulging his every whim and the culmination of this was the House of the Senses Awakened. A maze-like pleasure palace located in the wilds of the Porthmos Gap, Cydonus III effectively moved Taldor's capital from Oppara to this lavish pleasure ground during his reign. This included his harem of lovers and one in particular, Origen Sarlu, was especially fond of the Grand Prince.

In the wake of the Even-Tongued Conquest, Grand Prince Cydonus III was poisoned by a Qadiran agent (later identified by the Ballingar Accord). After the Grand Prince's death, Origen Sarlu absconded with his body and sealed himself, the Grand Prince's body, and a significant portion of Taldor's wealth within the House of the Senses Awakened, turning the complex into a trap-riddled hazard that has remained impenetrable to this day. Origen Sarlu still dwells within, having become a powerful form of undead known as a nemhain. The house is also protected by an array of other undead, including spectres, wights, and wraiths, as well as ancient stone golems.
1770387992:
Bellis

Bellis is a remote settlement in far northeastern Andoran along the banks of the Sellen River. Established as a lumber town, it now produces additional agricultural goods in addition to cut timber. Bellisians like to refer to themselves as "foresters" and regard "lumberjacks" as undisciplined amateurs.
1796348972:
Wrathwarped Halls

The Wrathwarped Halls were a Bakrakhan research complex beneath the Calphiak Mountains, dedicated to the study and creation of new weapons and soldiers for use in the wars against Shalast. In the modern era, it was forgotten until goblins from the Rocksmashers tribe discovered the site while rolling their iconic boulder totem. The goblins seized the site, using its laboratories to enhance their capabilities, and began plotting their conquest of the other tribes in the area. Shoantis discovered the location in 4717 AR and requested the Pathfinder Society's assistance in investigating the ruins. The energies of fury were sealed away by a priest from Soralyon who had heard about the ruins.
1799015962:
Great Tomb of Leadership

1 locations:
  • Grand Cathedral of Pharasma
1803340422:
Wyo's Rest

Wyo's Rest is a small Qadiran island located off the Alavah Peninsula, nestled in the waters between Sedeq and the Meraz Desert.

Wyo's Rest is named after Lady Wyo, a powerful siren who rules the island. Lady Wyo is accompanied by a harem of male suitors provided by the city of Sedeq who all spend four years living a life of indentured luxury. In exchange, Lady Wyo guards the island and allows Qadiran ships to pass, even helping any vessels in distress.

The island is covered in both verdant plant life and elaborately designed architecture.
1818054952:
Redburrow

Redburrow was a mining hamlet in the Hollow Hills specializing in iron ore mining. Several mine shafts surrounded the settlement, providing some defense against attacks, but in 4717 AR the Ironfang Legion overwhelmed the defenders, burning the houses and its inhabitants to the ground.
1822747342:
Wingless Rock

Wingless Rock is a small, sparsely vegetated island off the west coast of Nidal. Local fishermen avoid it because there is nothing to catch, and there are also strong superstitions about bad luck. The Aspis Consortium used the island for smuggling artifacts from Nidal, but the operation was shut down by the Pathfinder Society.

What few people know is that beneath the island lie laboratories dedicated to Conference Z research.
1825300602:
Zhobl

Zhobl is a volcano located in the eastern Zho Mountains of Qadira. The eruptions of Zhobl and its companion Zhonar covered the nearby town of Shadun in ash and dust, forcing its inhabitants to abandon it.
1829577022:
Te Taspeh Valley

Te Taspeh Valley is a peaceful land dotted with the ruins of ancient wooden homes believed to be the surviving remnants of Mendev's once-nomadic society. Unusual carvings associate these buildings with these ancient people, who were believed to settle only in locations of great spiritual importance.

As of 4725 AR the valley's ruins had yet to be investigated in large part due to the possessive behavior of a nearby adamantine dragon.
1832554962:
Arkona Palace

Arkona Palace, or Palace Arkona, is a massive black manor atop the highest point of Endrin Isle that as of 4708 AR housed Old Korvosa's ruling family, House Arkona. Surrounded by an iron-wrought and black-marbled wall, the manor is adorned with strange ornamentation hailing from Vudra, such as doors bearing depictions of Chamidu and decor of elephants with three heads, peacocks holding sabers, and tigers with carved eyes that leer from the palace's walls and over the gardens of the mansion. Ruthless guards patrol the palace.

Beneath Arkona Palace is a famous cyst drop, an occurrence when a sea cave collapses in on itself to form a sinkhole. The cyst drop is formed from the natural caves that compose the Vaults of Korvosa. The palace's underground levels also include the Vivified Labyrinth, a sprawling and complex obstacle course and dungeon.
1842125522:
Fairy nest

In the northern Narlmarches, two fey creatures inhabit a fairy nest built roughly 40-feet high up in a fir tree's branches. The friendly duo, a cat-sized grig named Tyg-Titter-Tut and her little faerie dragon Perlivash, revel in pranking, teasing and scaring any humans they come across. They keep themselves entertained by quietly following people for days on end and taking turns tricking them with minor spells.

Given a good enough reason, however, their pranks can take a deadly turn. After observing the cruelty of the notorious trapper Breeg Orlivanch, one of his deadfall traps was triggered early by Perlivash. The incomplete trap collapsed, killing Breeg in the process.
1854516442:
Rookwarden

Located in the Loric Fells, Rookwarden is a stone and granite fortress built by dwarves in 4636 AR. Situated on the bluff of a quarry rich with marble, this fortress is easily defended by even a small number of soldiers. According to rumor, a secret network of tunnels honeycombs beneath the fortress and allegedly leads to entrances that could be used to take the fortress without an extended siege.

The history of the fortress is closely tied with the history of Loric Fells, with a rich history of occupation, from dwarves to humans to goblins and trolls.
1872042392:
Marian Leigh

Marian Leigh is a settlement in the southern portion of Ustalav's Canterwall county, close to Virlych's dangerous Hungry Mountains and on the northern banks of the Orphield River. Despite the proximity to such inherent evil, the town is one of the most pleasant in Ustalav and boasts a female to male ratio of ten to one. The town is centred on the garden estate of Lady Urora Demerrval and incorporates many greenhouses and gardens. The only suspicion overhanging the town is the disturbing and unexplained disappearances of some of the male visitors to the community.
1881223842:
Orcmoot

The Orcmoot is the yearly meeting near the dromaar town of Averaka on the remote island of the Flintyreach in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. At Orcmoot, the residents discuss their goals and visions for the island. Suota Iron-Tongue is the moderator of the Orcmoot.
1888256712:
Gholinom

Gholinom is a secret alghollthu factory-city hidden in an oceanic trench west of Rahadoum.
1908973022:
Maheto

Maheto is a highly-defended Taldan city located in the northern foothills of the World's Edge Mountains. This settlement is home to highly-skilled dwarven craftsmen whose blades and other products are in high demand by members of the empire's ruling class.

As a symbol of their allegiance to Taldor the legendary sword Turlang was forged and given to Grand Prince Arthune. This blade commiserates the slaying of an ancient dragon named Tharularg and is a masterpiece of Dwarven craftsmanship.
1922288362:
Vurnirn

Vurnirn is a centaur settlement situated in the north-east of the Hoofwood, in the region of Iobaria known as the Dvezda Marches. It acts a meeting place for the various centaur tribes. It was founded in 1466 AR.
1929480132:
Misery Row

Misery Row is a street running along the eastern edge of the Coins district of Absalom, and was until recently was the only street in the city where the slave trade was always legal. Slavery was abolished in Absalom following the events of the Fiendflesh Siege of 4717 AR.
1941346612:
Black Squares of Ungevick

The Black Squares of Ungevick are one of the cyclopean monuments of ancient Thassilon, built with the labor of enslaved rune and stone giants. They are located off the southern coast of modern Varisia, west of modern-day Korvosa in what was the Thassilonian nation of Eurythnia.

The squares are believed to have been one component of a necromantic engine built by Zutha, and remains haunted by incorporeal undead.
1960388002:
Seredain

Seredain is a large town in the River Kingdom of Touvette. It is the site of one of Touvette's three charity buildings, along with the capital of Avendale and the town of Voluse.
1965905042:
No-Horn

The No-Horn is a low quality tavern in Magnimar's Dockway neighbourhood. It typifies the sort of establishment that pops up periodically near the Bazaar of Sails, only to close again in short order. It is named for its sign, which depicts a hornless unicorn being tormented by drunken devils.

The structure itself is a rickety and leaning thing constructed from an old ship's hull. The establishment's barkeep is an ornery man missing both of his ears. Patrons can expect cheap food and drink here, but both are of an extraordinarily low quality.
2014876942:
Tathandyr

Tathandyr is an elven town nestled in the foothills of the Five Kings Mountains in the far north of that country on the border with Kyonin. The town was founded by the mystic Eninaleryl, who lends her aid in sacred rituals and ceremonies.

The proximity to the ruined city of Shevaroth presents a challenge for traversing the region, though the presence of the Green Faith helps combat demonic corruption. The druids say that an artifact capable of fighting corruption lies beneath the town.
2043583222:
Umnyango

Umnyango, known as Kalabuto during colonial times, is the largest settlement in Vidrian. It is a crumbling city built by Mwangi tribesmen in the pre-colonial era, and the overwhelming majority of the inhabitants are Mwangi who, until 4717 AR, were ruled over by a minority of Sargavan colonialists. It was the main target for the anti-colonial sentiments of the Mwangi city of Mzali to the east, and in time Mwangi both inside and outside of Umnyango united to expel the Sargavans from the city.
2044031132:
Raketooth

Raketooth is the second-largest port on Shark Island in the Shackles. It is a dreary settlement specializing in pilfered luxury wares that are usually sold on Motaku Isle or to the few non-slaver merchants who come to Shark Island. Raketooth rarely has difficulties with sedacthys who plague the island. The pirate lords of Raketooth, Mauril Breakwater and "Baron" Venigo Palpathe, maintain the relative peace of the town by providing tribute to the nearby sedacthy tribes.
2053163692:
Niishan

Niishan is a small town southwest of Lake Starfall in the nation of Degasi. It is known for celebrating the weeklong Festival of Fire holiday for millennia in honor of the town's founding and prosperity.
2057065402:
Beragel's Fur and Feathers

1 locations:
  • House of Guile
2057956642:
Ngozu

The picturesque port of Ngozu sits on the long cove that gave Mgange Cove island its name.

Ngozu is home to a sizeable fleet of pirate vessels crewed by sailors of Mwangi heritage, nearly all of Bonuwat descent. Residents of the town are loyal devotees of the ancient Mwangi faith of juju, a religion committed to congress with the spirit world of wendo. Ajuoga Baas is the chief wendifa and ruler of the settlement.

Ngozu tolerates non-Mwangi ships, but Ngozu's rules are strict for non-natives, and most laws involve "blood price" punishments.
2073506202:
Battle of Screaming Tree

The Battle of Screaming Tree is a historic conflict that took place in the eastern Blood Plains of the Hold of Belkzen, just west of the Flood Road in 4051 AR. It forever changed the region and the history of the struggle over central Belkzen. The Screaming Tree, now located at this site, serves as a constant reminder of the pain wrought on this battlefield.
2077851472:
Mystic's Redoubt

Mystic's Redoubt is the only public arcane library in Port Peril. It is a six-winged circular tower in Crescent Harbor, and it charges fees of as much as 1,000 gold pieces per year for its use. A small number of apprentices join the library each year, while other scholars visit Mystic's Redoubt to study relics in its antiquities hall. Rare natural components also grow in an adjacent, well-maintained garden.
2086563492:
White Canyon

White Canyon is not a single passage but a network of branching gulfs whose walls narrow overhead. Kholo live in hide tents in the most sheltered areas, or dig burrows at the base of the canyon walls. Rath Sandstalker conducts frequent raids on caravans and small settlements, seeking healthy humanoids to sell at the Fleshfairs. The warriors of Fort Longjaw attempt an attack from time to time.

The White Canyon tribe is the second-largest kholo hold in Katapesh.

Despite the lingering fear many people have of the monstrous population here, kholo have not been a unified threat since the Siege of Solku, when the tribe assailed the city of Solku. Even so persistent rumours speak of Noor, the Red Sultana, who is said to be attempting to gather the kholo under her banner for another assault on the city, and then beyond.
2089662602:
Devil's Drain

Devil's Drain is a dangerous whirlpool in the Steaming Sea located between the coasts of Southmoor and the Ironbound Island of Dragon's Rib. On a good day there is just enough room either side of the Devil's Drain for a ship to pass through, though it is always treacherous. Using this route saves enough time on the passage to Varisia that desperate captains will still sometimes risk it.

Devil's Drain appears to be a natural occurrence formed by oceanic currents but adventurers who have returned from its depths report mysterious deep rifts in the ocean floor below it. To make matters worse, the area surrounding Devil's Drain has at times been home to dragon turtles, fjord linnorms, scyllas, and krakens who often drive ships into the whirlpool to feast on the shipwrecked sailors.
2095801562:
Sezgin

Sezgin is a small town in the River Kingdom of Lambreth.
2100747822:
Hocum's Fantasmagorium

1 locations:
  • Kelimber's Dry Goods and Supplies
2111618392:
Pol

Pol is the capital of the Taldan prefecture of Whitemarch after the previous capital of Cydonus was destroyed during the Grand Campaign, Taldor's last war with neighbouring Qadira. Pol remains a work in progress, with much of the settlement existing only on the drawing board of its architects. Pol serves more as a crossroads between the Monastery of the Seven Forms and various settlements and military bases across the rest of the Whitemarch Prefecture.

It is also a source of contention amongst the local aristocracy who vie mercilessly for who gets the rights to build what where within the city, for influence within its burgeoning local political scene, and ultimately for who will rule this new city.
2112417102:
Grandfather's Arms

3 locations:

  • Wall of Faces
  • Weeping Grandfather
2123292432:
Breachill

7 locations:

2138884392:
Gunworks

The Gunworks of Alkenstar was built in 4620 AR and has been the primary source of firearms in the Grand Duchy and the rest of Golarion ever since.
